key_insight: SFBench introduces a new benchmark for judging whether scientific materials-science claims are feasible, using expert-annotated, de novo claims and open-ended explanations.
engineering_relevance: It provides a more realistic test for AI systems meant to triage, validate, or prioritize scientific ideas before costly experimental or simulation work.
method: Each claim labeled by subject-matter experts on a 5-point feasibility scale
Annotations include open-ended explanations plus evaluation metrics and baseline GPT results
evidence_signal: The benchmark uses 197 expert-annotated claims with ground-truth feasibility scores on a five-point scale and explanation-based evaluation.
watch_item: Because the benchmark is relatively small and focused on materials science, performance may not generalize to broader scientific domains or to unseen reasoning styles.
paper_title: SFBench: The SciFy Scientific Feasibility Benchmark
